other_observers: Nick Komar and Eric Defonso; I don't know who reported it, but it had been seen before we I saw it.
specific_location: Chico Basin Ranch, It is about 1 mile south of Hanover. Then there is an area with a lot of trees and Russian Olives and that is where it was hanging out.
description: Black on the head, back, and tail. White patch on the base of the pimaries. A few white patches on the secondaries. Rufous on the sides of the birds and undertail covert. White belly. Red eye.Behaviors: It acted towhee like. It usually stayed down low in dense brush. When we first saw it, it was sitting up in the top of some bushes. Then it flew off after about 5 minutes and stayed low in the Russian Olives for the rest of the times we were around that area, which was about 30 minutes. We would see it every once in a while during that 30 minutes when it decided to fly from one bush to another.Call: A nasal zewheekPlumage: Adult
